results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THIS PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP AN EXPOSOME BASED APPROACH THAT CHARACTERIZES CHEMICAL AND NON-CHEMICAL STRESSORS TO PROVIDE DATA ON EARLY LIFE STAGE VULNERABILITIES IN AGRICULTURAL COMMUNITIES.ACTIVITIES:THE OBJECTIVES OF THIS PROJECT ARE THE 1) ASSESSMENT OF PESTICIDE DATA TO CHARACTERIZE CHILD EXPOSURE, GENETIC POLYMORPHISMS, HAZARD VALUES, 2) INTEGRATION OF COHORT BIOMARKERS INTO THE EXPOSOME BASED FRAMEWORK, 3) IDENTIFICATION OF CUMULATIVE EXPOSURE IMPACTS IN CHILDREN, AND 4) QUANTIFIED COMPARISONS OF BOTH NATIONWIDE AND STATE-SPECIFIC ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE MAPPING TOOLS.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:THE EXPECTED OUTCOMES OF THIS PROJECT WILL BE A DEVELOPMENTAL CENTER BASED ON EARLY LIFE-STAGE HEALTH STRESSORS AND NEW SCIENTIFIC UNDERSTANDINGS OF THE EFFECTS OF EXPOSURE TO CHEMICAL AND NON-CHEMICAL STRESSORS THROUGH AN EXPOSOME BASED APPROACH. DELIVERABLES WILL INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS DOCUMENTING THE CHILDREN'S AGRICULTURAL COHORT INTEGRATIVE BIOMARKER ASSAYS IN RESPONSE TO ENVIRONMENTAL STRESSORS, CHARACTERIZATION OF COHORT EXPOSOME, DETAILED STATE AND REGION-SPECIFIC MAPS COMPARING MAPPING TOOLS, AND RECOMMENDATIONS FOR IMPROVED FUTURE CUMULATIVE IMPACT MAPPING FRAMEWORKS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS PROJECT INCLUDE RURAL AND AGRICULTURAL COMMUNITIES, FARM WORKERS, STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
